message: Derived atom returns a new object/array literal on every read. Each evaluation of atom(get => ({ ... })) produces a new reference, so every subscriber re-renders even when underlying values are unchanged. Use selectAtom with an equality function, or stabilize the shape with atom(get => derived, equal) to avoid unnecessary re-renders.

Derived atom returns a new object/array literal on every read. Each evaluation of atom(get => ({ ... })) produces a new reference, so every subscriber re-renders even when underlying values are unchanged. Use selectAtom with an equality function, or stabilize the shape with atom(get => derived, equal) to avoid unnecessary re-renders.

Derived atom returns a new object/array literal on every read. Each evaluation of atom(get => ({ ... })) produces a new reference, so every subscriber re-renders even when underlying values are unchanged. Use selectAtom with an equality function, or stabilize the shape with atom(get => derived, equal) to avoid unnecessary re-renders.

selectAtom() is created inside a component render body. That recreates the selector on every render and weakens Jotai memoization. Hoist the selector to module scope or extract it outside the component.

selectAtom() is created inside a component render body. That recreates the selector on every render and weakens Jotai memoization. Hoist the selector to module scope or extract it outside the component.

selectAtom selector returns an object or array literal without an equality function (3rd argument). Each evaluation creates a new reference, causing downstream atoms/subscribers to re-render infinitely. Add a deep equality function as the third argument (e.g., shallowEqual).

selectAtom selector returns an object or array literal without an equality function (3rd argument). Each evaluation creates a new reference, causing downstream atoms/subscribers to re-render infinitely. Add a deep equality function as the third argument (e.g., shallowEqual).

This component subscribes to the full TanStack Query atom. That broad subscription causes unnecessary re-renders when only a slice is needed. Use selectAtom() to derive the minimal state the component actually reads.
